name: Elaine's Dolmas
recipeIngredient: 1/2 cup Long grain brown rice; cooked
recipeInstructions:
1. Place grape leaves briefly in a pan of warm water to separate them, then drain on paper towels.
2. Combine all other ingredients.
3. Place grape leaf vein side up, with the stem toward you.
4. Put a mound of rice mixture in the middle of the leaf (about 1 rounded teaspoon to 1 rounded tablespoon, depending on the size of the leaf).
5. Fold over sides and roll up leaf.
6. Layer the rolled leaves in a large saucepan (3 quart), placing them side by side and close together.
7. Press with a heavy heat-proof plate that fits inside the pan.
8. Add enough boiling water to cover the leaves.
9. Cover and simmer for 1½ hours.
Hints: Dolmas may be served warm or chilled. If warm, try serving them with lemon sauce. If cold, serve them with garbanzo puree or eggplant. Tabouli is also a good dish to serve at the same meal. Dolmas are easy to take to a potluck dinner and they also make a very special "company meal."
